title: tvbox app manifest
description: One app on the tvbox HOME screen. Lives at shell/apps/<id>.json (first-party) or ~/.tvbox/apps/<id>.json / ~/.tvbox/apps/<id>/manifest.json (user-installed). See docs/app-manifest.md.

localeString.description: A plain string (brand names) or a per-locale map, e.g. { "en": "Live TV", "hu": "Élő TV" }.

id.description: Unique app id. Also the tile focus key, the apps-data/<id> bundle dir, and the /<id>/ mount for non-root static apps.

manifestVersion.description: Manifest format version this file speaks. Omitted = 1. The shell skips manifests with a version it doesn't know.

version.description: The app's own version (semver). Bump it to offer users an Update in the store.

changelog.description: Release notes shown in the store's app detail view, newest version first. English only.

type.description: webclient = the shell serves/loads the app (a package's own local web/ bundle, the legacy root bundle, or a remote site). Apps are self-contained packages now; there are no launcher-compiled 'builtin' views.

status.description: coming_soon renders a non-launchable teaser tile.

accent.description: Tile accent color. Hex only - the shell drops anything else (it is interpolated into launcher CSS).

icon.description: Inline SVG markup for the tile. Rendered sandboxed (as an data: URI), so it cannot run script.

service.description: Shell-side plugin name. Repo apps resolve shell/plugins/<service>.js; a user app dir resolves its own plugin.js. Loaded only when requires.bin are all present.

requires.description: Runtime dependencies. Missing bins grey the tile ('needs X') instead of crashing anything.

bin.description: Binaries that must resolve on PATH (~/.tvbox/bin is on PATH) for the app to be launchable.

download.description: No-root static-binary installs for `tvbox deps <id>`: sha256-verified into ~/.tvbox/bin. Preferred over apt when upstream ships binaries.

extract.description: Path of the binary inside the archive (.tar.gz/.zip). Omit for a raw binary download or when it equals bin.

apt.description: Debian packages `tvbox deps <id>` installs (the one step that asks for sudo). Only used for bins download can't cover (e.g. mpv).

aptRepo.description: Optional third-party APT repo for the apt deps (e.g. raspotify). All fields are validated by the CLI before touching root.

disableService.description: System services `tvbox deps` disables after the apt install (e.g. raspotify's own daemon - the shell supervises librespot itself).

install.description: Web-client bundle install recipe (user-space, no root; runs from the UI or `tvbox install <id>`). The acquired files land in apps-data/<id>.

ref.description: flatpak: the app ref (e.g. tv.plex.PlexHTPC), installed --user from flathub.

arch.description: flatpak: ref arch (a web-client extract is arch-independent, so x86_64 is fine on a Pi).

url.description: url: a .tar.gz/.zip to download; git: a repo to shallow-clone.

extract.description: Subpath inside the acquired source that holds the web client.

match.description: strip-script: remove <script> tags whose attributes contain this substring from the entry HTML.

runtime.description: How the shell serves/launches the app and what the preload bridge exposes to it.

serve.description: local: a package app that ships its own web/ UI bundle, served at /<id>/ in the main window (the usual model - apps are packages). static: the legacy single root-mounted bundle (mount:root, e.g. Plex). remote: load a live site in an isolated, hardened window.

mount.description: static: mount at / (single root app per box) instead of /<id>/.

url.description: remote: the site to load (https, or http for private/LAN hosts).

urlConfig.description: remote: read the URL from the named config section's baseUrl instead of a literal url (for self-hosted services).

userAgent.description: remote: UA override (e.g. a smart-TV UA for youtube.com/tv).

origins.description: remote: hostnames (plus subdomains) navigation is allowed to. Defaults to the url's host. Also the allowlist for the `fetch` capability.

capabilities.description: THE security boundary: which preload-bridge surfaces the app gets. Defaults to ["nav"] - an app that forgets this must not silently gain player/config/fetch. `fetch` is scoped to `origins`.

bridge.description: Renderer bridge adapter from shell/bridges/<name>.js (e.g. qwebchannel for Plex HTPC).

player.description: mpv-fullscreen: video behind the transparent launcher. mpv-overlay: video behind a transparent app-declared element (transparentSelector).

transparentSelector.description: CSS selector of the app element made transparent to reveal mpv (e.g. Plex's #media-container).
